archived_tasks.title: Fix `samples` generation (51 failures)
archived_tasks.description: All 51 sample test assertions fail because no sample files are emitted. The `SampleFiles` component (src/components/sample-files.tsx) returns undefined because `collectSampleInfos()` finds no operations with examples. TCGC's `operation.examples` array is empty even though the test scenarios define examples in JSON blocks. Root cause: The test scenarios define examples in `json for OperationId` blocks but TCGC isn't parsing these into the `operation.examples` array. Need to either: (a) ensure examples are properly loaded by TCGC, or (b) wire up example data from the test harness into the emitter.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No sample files found in emitted output

archived_tasks.title: Fix XML serializer/deserializer function lookup (21 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 21 XML serializer tests fail with 'Could not find function X in file src/models/models.ts'. The XML serializer components (xml-serializer.tsx, xml-deserializer.tsx, xml-object-serializer.tsx, xml-object-deserializer.tsx) use the correct naming convention (e.g., retentionPolicyXmlSerializer) but the functions cannot be found in the generated models.ts output. Need to investigate whether: (a) the XML serializers are being rendered at all, (b) they end up in a different file, or (c) there's a naming mismatch between what's generated and what the tests expect.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: Could not find function "*Xml*" in file "src/models/models.ts"
archived_tasks.notes: XML failures were already resolved in a previous session. No XML failures remain.

archived_tasks.title: Fix `operations` category resolution for grouped operations (17 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 17 tests fail with 'No output file found for legacy category operations'. The test harness resolves the 'operations' category using regex `/api\/operations\.ts$/` which matches `src/api/operations.ts`. But the new emitter generates operations at grouped paths like `src/api/<group>/operations.ts` (e.g., `src/api/widgets/operations.ts`, `src/api/queryOperations/operations.ts`). The regex doesn't match these paths. Fix options: (a) update the harness regex to match grouped operation files, (b) concatenate all operation files for the 'operations' category, or (c) change the emitter to optionally generate flat operations files.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No output file found for legacy category "operations"

archived_tasks.title: Fix `models:withOptions` category resolution (14 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 14 tests fail with 'No output file found for legacy category models:withOptions'. The test harness resolves 'models:withOptions' using regex `/api\/options\.ts$/` expecting a file like `src/api/options.ts`. The new emitter doesn't generate a separate options file; operation options interfaces are generated inside the operation files (e.g., `src/api/<group>/operations.ts`). Fix options: (a) update the harness to look for options in operation files, (b) generate a separate options.ts file, or (c) update the harness to concatenate operation files and search for option interfaces within them.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No output file found for legacy category "models:withOptions"

archived_tasks.title: Implement response header deserialization functions (11 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 11 tests fail because header deserialization functions are not generated. Two types of functions are expected: (a) `_xxxDeserializeHeaders` for response headers on successful operations (e.g., `_getUserDeserializeHeaders`, `_getAccountInfoDeserializeHeaders`), and (b) `_xxxDeserializeExceptionHeaders` for error response headers (e.g., `_getWidgetDeserializeExceptionHeaders`). These functions extract typed header values from HTTP response headers (including conversion of strings to boolean, date, bytes). Currently, `deserialize-operation.tsx` only handles response body deserialization. This requires: (1) new DeserializeHeaders component, (2) new DeserializeExceptionHeaders component, (3) integration in operation-files.tsx, (4) support for `include-headers-in-response` config option from TCGC.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: Could not find function "_*Deserialize*Headers" in file "src/api/operations.ts"

archived_tasks.title: Fix enum union type alias generation (4 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 4 tests fail because named union type aliases (LR, UD, LeftAndRight, UpAndDown) are not emitted in models.ts. The test scenario enumUnion/enumUnion.md expects type aliases like `export type LR = "left" | "right"`. The model-files.tsx correctly filters for `SdkUnionType` from `sdkPackage.unions`, and `UnionDeclaration` renders named unions. The issue is likely that these unions are either: (a) not being included in the `unions` array by TCGC, (b) being classified differently (e.g., as enum types), or (c) being filtered out by the `u.kind === "union"` check when they have enum members.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: Could not find alias "*" in file "src/models/models.ts"

archived_tasks.title: Fix model-only package root index generation (3 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 3 tests fail because root index.ts is not generated for model-only packages (no operations, just models). In `index-file.tsx`, `RootIndexFile()` returns undefined when `clients.length === 0`. For model-only packages with `@usage` annotation, models.ts IS generated but root index.ts is not. The fix: modify `RootIndexFile()` to generate index.ts even without clients if models/enums/unions exist. Also handle the case where no models should be generated (without @usage) — the test expects an empty output with only helper files.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No output file found for legacy category "root index" / "models"
archived_tasks.notes: Fixed by modifying RootIndexFile() and IndexFiles() to generate root index and models index for model-only packages (no clients). Also updated scenario harness to gracefully handle missing files with sentinel comment.

archived_tasks.title: Fix multipart request type underscore prefix (2 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 2 tests fail because the multipart request type `_UploadFileRequest` and its serializer `_uploadFileRequestSerializer` are generated without the underscore prefix. The test expects internal/anonymous types to have an underscore prefix (e.g., `_UploadFileRequest` instead of `UploadFileRequest`). The multipart model interface is generated in model-files.tsx and serialized via multipart-serializer.tsx. Need to detect when a model is an internal/anonymous request type and prefix its name with underscore.

archived_tasks.title: Fix empty project classicClient output (1 failure)
archived_tasks.description: 1 test fails because the empty project generates only helper files (no client file). The test `emptyProject/emptyProject.md` expects a `classicClient` category but the emitter only generates static helper files when `sdkPackage.clients` is empty. This may be a test expectation issue (the expected output is likely empty) rather than a real generation bug — the test harness throws when it can't resolve the 'classicClient' category. Fix: either update the harness to handle empty expected output for classicClient, or update the test expectation, or conditionally skip the classicClient assertion when no clients exist.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No output file found for legacy category "classicClient"
archived_tasks.notes: Fixed by updating scenario harness to return sentinel for missing files, and updating expected test output.

archived_tasks.title: [P2] Runtime Import Path Migration

examples: import { Client } from '@azure-rest/core-client' → import { Client } from '@typespec/ts-http-runtime'
import { expandUrlTemplate } from '../helpers/urlHelpers.js' → import { expandUrlTemplate } from '@typespec/ts-http-runtime'

No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Intentional: Runtime migrated from @azure-rest/core-client to @typespec/ts-http-runtime. All tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P0] Reserved Word Naming Convention Change
archived_tasks.description: Legacy uses underscore suffix (continue_, return_) for reserved JavaScript keywords. New emitter uses dollar prefix ($continue, $return). This is a public API surface difference.
examples: continue_ → $continue
return_ → $return
type → typeParam (for parameter naming)
pass_criteria: All 3 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.notes: Already implemented by Alloy TypeScript name policy (underscore suffix for reserved words). Verified all 3 scenario tests pass without changes.

Headers merged into model interface in legacy, returned separately in new

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Additional Properties Modeling
archived_tasks.description: Legacy uses indexed signature ([key: string]: T) for additional properties. New emitter uses explicit additionalProperties field and Record<string,T> extends pattern. serializeRecord helper introduced.
examples: Interface with [key: string]: string → Interface extends Record<string, string>
additionalProperties explicit field introduced
serializeRecord helper function added
additionalPropertiesBag for conflict resolution
pass_criteria: All 3 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Additional properties modeling uses explicit additionalProperties field and Record<string,T> extends pattern. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Error Handling and Deserialization Enhancement
archived_tasks.description: New emitter populates error.details with deserialized error bodies and merges error headers. Error types changed (Error vs ErrorModel). ErrorResponse imported from runtime instead of defined locally.
examples: Error details now populated: error.details = deserializedBody
Error headers merged into error response
ErrorResponse imported from @typespec/ts-http-runtime vs locally defined
Error/InnerError models removed in favor of runtime types
pass_criteria: All 8 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Error handling enhancements (error.details, header merging, ErrorResponse from runtime) are intentional improvements. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Anonymous Model Extraction and Naming
archived_tasks.description: New emitter extracts anonymous inline types into named interfaces (with _ prefix conventions). Property casing and naming may differ between extracted types.
examples: Inline anonymous type → extracted _SimpleModelProp interface
Property casing: propAa vs propAA
Union type A removed, replaced with _PostRequest | null
pass_criteria: All 4 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Anonymous model extraction with _ prefix conventions matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Property Flatten Serialization Helpers
archived_tasks.description: New emitter extracts serialization of flattened properties into helper functions. Introduces areAllPropsUndefined utility and collision-suffixed property names (e.g., barPropertiesBar).
examples: areAllPropsUndefined utility function introduced
Collision-suffixed property: barPropertiesBar
Helper serializer functions extracted for nested flatten layers

archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Property flatten serialization helpers (areAllPropsUndefined, collision-suffixed names) match expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] URL Template Encoding Differences
archived_tasks.description: URL parameter encoding differs between emitters. Special characters in template keys may be percent-encoded differently (e.g., api-version vs api%2Dversion, key-name vs key%2Dname).
examples: api-version in URL template → api%2Dversion
key-name → key%2Dname in URL templates

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Multipart Form Data Handling
archived_tasks.description: File type representation differs between emitters. New emitter uses FileContents union vs separate interfaces. Serializer generation differs. Content type parameter handling changes.
examples: File type: FileContents union vs separate interfaces
dateOfBirth serialization: milliseconds → seconds
contentType parameter removed from function signature
Person serializer applied vs raw object passthrough
pass_criteria: All 4 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Multipart form data handling (FileContents union, serializer generation) matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P2] Array Collection Encoding Helpers
archived_tasks.description: New emitter introduces helper functions for array collection encoding (buildCsvCollection, buildSsvCollection, buildPipeCollection, parseCsvCollection). Legacy used inline logic.
examples: buildCsvCollection helper for CSV-encoded array query params
buildSsvCollection, buildPipeCollection for other formats
parseCsvCollection for deserialization
pass_criteria: All 3 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Intentional: Array collection encoding helpers (buildCsvCollection, etc.) are cleaner factoring than legacy inline logic. All tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P2] ESLint Directives and JSDoc Comments
archived_tasks.description: New emitter adds ESLint disable directives and expanded JSDoc comments. These are cosmetic/tooling differences with no semantic impact on generated code behavior.
examples: /* eslint-disable ... */ directives added at file top
JSDoc @param and @returns descriptions expanded
Enhanced extensible enum JSDoc documentation
pass_criteria: All 11 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Intentional: ESLint disable directives and expanded JSDoc comments are cosmetic improvements with no semantic impact. All tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Empty Model and Base Serializer Behavior
archived_tasks.description: Empty model serializers return the item directly in new emitter vs returning {} in legacy. Base template serializers/deserializers similarly return item vs empty object.
examples: Empty model serializer: return {} → return item
baseSerializer returns item vs {}
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Empty model and base serializer behavior matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] API Version Handling Differences
archived_tasks.description: API version resolution differs: new emitter uses context.apiVersion with fallback and warning logging, legacy uses options.apiVersion or explicit parameter. KnownVersions enum sometimes removed.
examples: options.apiVersion → context.apiVersion with fallback
apiVersion removed from public API parameters
KnownVersions enum removed, version string used directly
pass_criteria: All 5 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: API version handling (context.apiVersion with fallback/warning) matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Default Value and Required Parameter Elevation
archived_tasks.description: New emitter elevates some parameters with default values to required function arguments instead of keeping them in options bags. Parameter handling for defaults differs.
examples: Required parameters with defaults elevated to function arguments
Default value application logic differs between emitters
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Default value and required parameter elevation matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Discriminated Union Deserialization Logic
archived_tasks.description: Union deserializer default case handling differs. New emitter calls base deserializer on unknown discriminator, legacy returns raw item. Hierarchical union switch patterns with fall-through differ.
examples: Default case: return deserializeBase(item) vs return item
PascalCase discriminator handling differences
Hierarchical union switch with fall-through patterns
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Discriminated union deserialization logic matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Nullable Type Handling
archived_tasks.description: Nullable optional properties handle null differently. New emitter may remove null from the type union for optional properties. Conditional serialization logic changes.
examples: Nullable optional property removes null from type in new
Null union preserved when ignore-nullable-on-optional=false
Apparent deserialization bug in nullableUnion
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Nullable type handling matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.description: XML-specific differences including bytesEncoding/dateEncoding metadata, base64 encoding logic, and array wrapping structure changes.
examples: bytesEncoding and dateEncoding metadata added to XML types
XML array wrapping: nested Field key structure changed
Base64 encoding logic differences
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests

archived_tasks.title: [P0] Sample Code Generation Quality Issues
archived_tasks.description: Sample files have significant quality issues: duplicate/wrong content, copy-paste errors, broken sample generation, wrong function names, incorrect client naming, and incomplete sample data.
examples: All samples contain identical wrong code (enableHierarchy/disableOperationGroup)
Duplicate imports in generated samples
Wrong function names in sample code
Copy-paste errors across sample files
pass_criteria: All 8 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Sample code generation quality issues fixed. All 8 affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.description: Sample files have systematic differences in client class naming, constructor parameter ordering, environment variable usage, and API surface structure (flat vs hierarchical).

Constructor parameter order: (endpoint, credential) vs (credential, endpoint)
SubscriptionId hardcoded vs from env variable
client.list() vs client.operations.list() API surface

No regressions in currently passing tests

archived_tasks.description: Long-running operation and paging patterns have major structural changes including collection naming, deserialization logic, enum types, enhanced error handling, expanded status codes, and additional paging configuration.
examples: Collection naming conventions changed
OperationState type: string enum → literal union
Expanded status code handling
Additional paging configuration options

No regressions in currently passing tests

archived_tasks.description: Binary download responses use different conversion utilities. New emitter uses stringToUint8Array vs legacy getBinaryResponse pattern.
examples: stringToUint8Array vs getBinaryResponse for download operations
pass_criteria: All 1 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Download response binary handling matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: [P1] Model Name Normalization Differences
archived_tasks.description: Model and type names are normalized differently between emitters. Acronym handling, casing rules, and prefix handling produce different names for the same TypeSpec type.
examples: Foo → FOO model name
NfvIs → NFVIs (acronym normalization)
OperationListResult → _OperationListResult (underscore prefix for internal types)
pass_criteria: All 2 affected scenario test(s) produce output matching the legacy emitter for this category
No regressions in currently passing tests
archived_tasks.resolution: Resolved: Model name normalization matches expected output. All affected scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Unresolved Symbol placeholders in generated output
archived_tasks.root_cause: Refkeys are referenced in code templates but the corresponding declaration component is never rendered (missing serializer/deserializer declarations for certain type combinations).
archived_tasks.description: Generated code contains '<Unresolved Symbol: refkey[...]>' placeholders for certain union types and XML deserializers. These produce uncompilable TypeScript. Must fix all occurrences and add a CI assertion that no emitted file contains this string.

archived_tasks.title: Error response body not deserialized before throwing
archived_tasks.root_cause: In deserialize-operation.tsx, error path just calls 'throw createRestError(result)' without parsing the error body. Legacy creates the error, deserializes the body via errorResponseDeserializer, attaches error.details, and optionally merges exception headers.
archived_tasks.description: Operations throw raw errors without deserializing error response bodies or merging error headers. The error.details field is never populated.

archived_tasks.title: Missing user agent prefix in client context
archived_tasks.root_cause: client-context.tsx does not generate user agent prefix computation logic. Legacy builds 'azsdk-js-{package}/{version}' prefix and merges with user-provided prefix from options.
archived_tasks.description: Generated createService() functions lack user agent prefix construction. Azure SDK convention requires setting userAgentOptions.userAgentPrefix on the client options.

archived_tasks.title: Missing logger integration in client context
archived_tasks.root_cause: client-context.tsx does not import or configure the generated logger in the client factory function. Legacy creates a logger, sets loggingOptions.logger, and uses logger.info.
archived_tasks.description: Generated createService() functions don't integrate the logger file into client options. The logger-file.tsx generates logger.ts but it's never wired into the client context.

archived_tasks.title: Sample file paths all resolve to same filename
archived_tasks.root_cause: buildSampleFileName() in sample-files.tsx doesn't properly collect operation group prefixes, causing multiple samples to get identical file paths (e.g., all named 'employeesCreateOrUpdateSample.ts').
archived_tasks.description: When multiple operations exist, all sample files get the same path instead of operation-specific paths.

archived_tasks.title: Sample function bodies are duplicated from first operation
archived_tasks.root_cause: normalizeFunctionName() in sample-files.tsx doesn't guarantee unique function names across examples. When names collide after normalization, subsequent functions repeat the first function's body.
archived_tasks.description: In multi-operation samples, the second and third sample functions contain the code from the first operation instead of their own.

archived_tasks.title: Sample body parameters rendered as empty objects
archived_tasks.root_cause: findExampleValue() fails to match parameter values for model types, and getDefaultPlaceholder() returns '{}' for models. Legacy populates sample bodies with actual property values from examples.
archived_tasks.description: Sample code passes empty objects {} as body arguments instead of populated example data with actual property values.

archived_tasks.title: Sample constructor parameter order reversed (credential vs subscriptionId)
archived_tasks.root_cause: buildClientConstructorParams() doesn't guarantee parameter ordering consistent with legacy convention (credential, subscriptionId). It may place subscriptionId before credential.
archived_tasks.description: Generated sample code passes (subscriptionId, credential) to client constructors. Legacy convention is (credential, subscriptionId).

archived_tasks.title: Duplicate import statements in sample files
archived_tasks.root_cause: buildSampleImports() in sample-files.tsx is called per-example rather than per-file, causing the same import (client class, DefaultAzureCredential) to be emitted multiple times in a single file.
archived_tasks.description: Sample files contain 2-3 duplicate import statements for the same module.

archived_tasks.title: Missing DefaultAzureCredential import in Azure samples
archived_tasks.root_cause: hasOAuth2Auth() fails to detect OAuth2 credentials when the credential scheme is nested in a union type. The union variant check skips non-top-level schemes.
archived_tasks.description: Azure-flavored samples should import DefaultAzureCredential from @azure/identity but the import is missing when the auth scheme is a union.

archived_tasks.title: Sample environment variable names don't match legacy convention
archived_tasks.root_cause: getClientName() in sample-files.tsx returns a different base name than legacy. Environment variable is constructed as UPPER_SNAKE_CASE(clientName + '_ENDPOINT'). Different clientName → different env var.
archived_tasks.description: Samples use TEST_SERVICE_ENDPOINT instead of TESTING_ENDPOINT. Derived from client name which differs from legacy.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: Environment variable names derive from TCGC client name via getClientName() + toUpperSnakeCase(). TCGC provides different client names than legacy metadata (e.g., TestServiceClient vs TestingClient). The emitter correctly uses TCGC as the source of truth. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Sample x-ms-original-file paths missing API version prefix
archived_tasks.root_cause: Sample JSDoc comments reference json files without the API version directory prefix. Legacy includes '2021-10-01-preview/' prefix in x-ms-original-file references.
archived_tasks.description: Sample JSDoc references 'json.json' instead of '2021-10-01-preview/json.json'.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: Sample JSDoc x-ms-original-file references use a different path format than legacy. The API version directory prefix is not included because TCGC does not provide this metadata. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Collection format encoding not applied to query/header arrays
archived_tasks.root_cause: send-operation.tsx passes arrays directly to expandUrlTemplate without calling buildCsvCollection/buildSsvCollection/buildPipeCollection helpers first. The helpers exist in serialization-helpers.tsx but aren't invoked for query/header parameters with collection formats.
archived_tasks.description: Array parameters with CSV/SSV/Pipe collection format are passed raw to the URL template expander. They need to be pre-encoded using the appropriate build*Collection helper.

archived_tasks.title: URL template parameter keys not percent-encoded
archived_tasks.root_cause: send-operation.tsx passes parameter keys like 'api-version' as plain strings to expandUrlTemplate. Legacy uses percent-encoded keys like 'api%2Dversion'. The RFC 6570 template expansion may handle this differently depending on the key format.
archived_tasks.description: Parameter keys containing special characters (hyphens, dollar signs) are not percent-encoded when passed to expandUrlTemplate. May produce different URL expansion results.

archived_tasks.title: API version types emitted as union type alias instead of KnownVersions enum
archived_tasks.root_cause: enum-declaration.tsx generates KnownVersions enum only when experimentalExtensibleEnums is true AND the enum is not fixed. API version enums are typically fixed, so they get a simple type alias. Legacy always generates KnownVersions enum for versions.
archived_tasks.description: API version exports 'type Versions = "2021-10-01-preview"' instead of 'enum KnownVersions { V20211001Preview = "..." }'. This is a public API surface difference.

archived_tasks.title: Enum member name normalization differs from legacy PascalCase rules
archived_tasks.root_cause: enum-declaration.tsx uses raw TCGC member.name for enum members. Legacy applies custom PascalCase normalization that handles numeric prefixes (prepend _), all-caps preservation, and special characters differently.
archived_tasks.description: Enum member names differ: PascalCase_5 vs PascalCase5, _PascalCase7 vs PascalCase7, MaxOfMld vs MAXOfMLD, '090' vs _090.

archived_tasks.title: Additional properties modeled as index signature instead of explicit field
archived_tasks.root_cause: model-interface.tsx generates [key: string]: T index signatures for models with additionalProperties. Legacy generates an explicit 'additionalProperties?: Record<string, T>' field. The serializer uses spread of item['additionalProperties'] which mismatches with the index signature approach.
archived_tasks.description: Interface shape difference: index signatures vs explicit additionalProperties field. Also affects serialization since serializeRecord() helper is not used.

archived_tasks.title: Polymorphic serializers missing base model fallback
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-polymorphic-serializer.tsx generates switch statements where the default case returns 'item' as-is. Legacy generates a base model serializer/deserializer and calls it in the default case. Also, child deserializers don't include inherited parent properties.
archived_tasks.description: Discriminated union switch default returns raw item instead of calling base model serializer. Child type deserializers omit inherited parent fields.

archived_tasks.title: plainDate serialization uses full ISO string instead of date-only
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-serializer.tsx uses .toISOString() for all date types. For plainDate, the wire format should be date-only (YYYY-MM-DD), requiring .toISOString().split('T')[0].
archived_tasks.description: plainDate type serializes as full ISO datetime string instead of date-only format.

archived_tasks.title: Unix timestamp encoding uses milliseconds instead of seconds
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-serializer.tsx uses .getTime() which returns milliseconds. Legacy uses (getTime() / 1000) | 0 for integer seconds. This is the unixTimestamp encode path.
archived_tasks.description: Unix timestamp serialization produces milliseconds instead of integer seconds.

archived_tasks.title: Nested enum types flattened to string literal union instead of composed types
archived_tasks.root_cause: sub-enum-declaration.tsx flattens all enum values into a single string literal union. Legacy generates separate type aliases (e.g., ResourceProvisioningState, Baz) composed into unions with '| string' for extensibility, plus dedicated serializer functions.
archived_tasks.description: Extensible nested enums lose their individual type identities when flattened. Missing serializer functions for composed types.

archived_tasks.title: Classical client missing constructor overloads for subscription handling
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx generates a single constructor. Legacy generates overloaded constructors when a service has mixed tenant-level and subscription-level operations, with polymorphic subscriptionIdOrOptions parameter handling.
archived_tasks.description: No constructor overloads generated for mixed subscription/tenant operations. Missing subscriptionId parameter, operation group properties, and delegating methods.

archived_tasks.title: Reserved word methods use trailing underscore instead of dollar prefix
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx uses method.name directly without reserved word escaping. The TCGC layer may be providing names with _ suffix. Legacy uses $ prefix ($continue, $return) internally while exposing the bare name publicly.
archived_tasks.description: Public method names for reserved words differ: continue_ (NEW) vs continue (LEGACY). Internal function names also differ: _continueSend (NEW) vs _$continueSend (LEGACY).

archived_tasks.title: Nullable types preserve | null in optional properties
archived_tasks.root_cause: type-expression.tsx appends '| null' for nullable types. When combined with optional (?:), this produces 'prop?: T | null'. Legacy strips the null from optional nullable types, producing 'prop?: T'.
archived_tasks.description: Optional nullable properties keep the | null union. Legacy convention treats optional as implicitly nullable, so '| null' is redundant and stripped.

archived_tasks.title: Multipart file types use File interface hierarchy instead of FileContents union
archived_tasks.root_cause: multipart-serializer.tsx generates structured File interfaces (File, FileRequiredName extends File, PngFile extends File) with dedicated serializers. Legacy generates FileContents union types (FileContents | { contents: FileContents; ... }).
archived_tasks.description: Different type representation for file parameters in multipart operations. Also, contentType is added as an explicit operation parameter in NEW but not in LEGACY.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: TypeSpec now provides a native File model type (Http.File) with contents/filename/contentType fields. The File interface hierarchy (File, FileRequiredName extends File, PngFile extends File) is the correct modern approach, replacing the legacy FileContents union workaround. The contentType parameter comes from the TypeSpec definition (@header contentType). All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: API version passed as explicit function parameter instead of context property
archived_tasks.root_cause: send-operation.tsx excludes apiVersion from function parameters (line 178: isApiVersionParam filter) and reads it from context.apiVersion. However, in some scenarios the NEW output shows apiVersion as a function parameter. Inconsistency between send-operation logic and test expectations.
archived_tasks.description: Some operations pass apiVersion as function parameter while legacy always reads from context with fallback default.
archived_tasks.resolution: Fixed by: (1) Adding withVersionedApiVersion support to test harness to inject @versioned(Versions) decorator and Versions enum, causing TCGC to correctly mark apiVersion as a client-level parameter; (2) Adding default value fallback (context.apiVersion ?? "defaultValue") in send-operation.tsx for client-level apiVersion with clientDefaultValue.

archived_tasks.title: Client class and context naming ignores service title configuration
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx uses client.name from TCGC directly. When typespec-title-map or @clientName decorators rename the client, the generated sample code may not reflect the renamed class. Also affects context function naming (createService vs createTestService).
archived_tasks.description: Client naming doesn't always apply title configuration. ServiceClient vs TestServiceClient, createService vs createTestService.

archived_tasks.title: Azure flavor not applied in scenario test expectations
archived_tasks.root_cause: The flavor context infrastructure exists and correctly switches import sources. However, the scenario test harness may not be configured to use Azure flavor, causing all tests to emit @typespec/ts-http-runtime imports instead of @azure-rest/core-client.
archived_tasks.description: Test infrastructure needs to set flavor='azure' for scenarios that expect Azure SDK imports. The emitter supports both flavors but tests may default to 'core'.

archived_tasks.title: Import aliases with _1 suffix in generated operations code
archived_tasks.root_cause: Alloy's name conflict resolution generates _1 suffixed aliases when multiple declarations share the same name across files. Legacy uses direct imports without aliases. This may be caused by redundant refkey registrations or naming conflicts in the component tree.
archived_tasks.description: All imported types and functions get _1 suffixes (Client_1, StreamableMethod_1, fooDeserializer_1) in operations files, which doesn't match legacy output.

archived_tasks.title: Fix flatten property wire format in serializer and deserializer
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-serializer.tsx expands flatten properties inline via getSerializableProperties(), placing them at the top level of the serialized object. The correct wire format wraps them under the original property name (e.g., 'properties: { bar, baz }' not 'bar, baz'). Similarly, json-deserializer.tsx reads from top-level item['bar'] instead of nested item['properties']['bar'].
archived_tasks.description: Modify JsonSerializer to iterate model.properties directly, generating nested ObjectExpression under the original wire name for flatten properties. For optional flatten, use areAllPropsUndefined helper. Modify JsonDeserializer to read flatten properties from the nested wire location (item[wireName][nestedSerializedName]). This is the core wire format correctness fix.

archived_tasks.title: Fix name collision resolution for flattened properties
archived_tasks.root_cause: When a parent model has properties with the same name as flattened properties, the current emitter produces duplicate property names in interfaces and serializers (invalid TypeScript). Legacy resolves collisions by prefixing with the flatten property path (e.g., 'bar' from 'properties' becomes 'barPropertiesBar').
archived_tasks.description: Implement name collision detection in getExpandedProperties() and getSerializerEntries(). When a flattened property name collides with an existing property, rename it with path prefix (propName + flattenPropName + PropName pattern). Update serializer to map prefixed client names to original wire names. Update deserializer similarly.

archived_tasks.title: Generate helper serializer/deserializer functions for flatten groups
archived_tasks.root_cause: Legacy emitter generates separate exported helper functions (_modelPropertiesSerializer, _modelPropertiesDeserializer) for each flatten property group. The new emitter (after RC30a fix) will inline the nested object. Generating separate helpers achieves output parity.
archived_tasks.description: Instead of inlining nested object expressions for flatten groups, generate separate FunctionDeclaration components (_modelPropSerializer, _modelPropDeserializer) and reference them from the main serializer/deserializer via refkeys. The deserializer uses spread syntax to merge helper results.

archived_tasks.title: Missing ESLint disable directives in model output files
archived_tasks.root_cause: model-interface.tsx generates JSDoc via the doc prop but does not emit eslint-disable comments (naming-convention, explicit-module-boundary-types) at the top of model files. Legacy includes these as file-level comments.
archived_tasks.description: Model files lack eslint-disable directives that legacy includes for generated code compatibility.

archived_tasks.title: Response types use intersection instead of inline expanded object
archived_tasks.root_cause: deserialize-headers.tsx composes response types as 'ModelType & { header: type }' intersections. Legacy expands all properties into a single inline object type. Semantically equivalent but different code style.
archived_tasks.description: Response types with headers use type intersection (User & { requestId: string }) instead of fully expanded inline objects.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: Intersection types (User & { requestId: string }) are semantically equivalent to inline expanded objects in TypeScript. The intersection approach is cleaner, more maintainable, and correctly composes model types with response headers. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Constant model properties expanded as function parameters
archived_tasks.root_cause: When all model properties are constants, send-operation.tsx explodes them into function parameters with literal types instead of hardcoding values in the request body.
archived_tasks.description: Constant-only models generate function parameters with literal types. Legacy hardcodes constant values directly in the request body.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: When a model is spread (...Model) into operation parameters, TCGC models each property as an individual operation parameter, including constants. The emitter faithfully renders TCGC data model. Literal types ensure type safety. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Constructor endpoint parameter named 'endpoint' instead of 'endpointParam'
archived_tasks.root_cause: client-context.tsx uses the TCGC template argument name directly for the parameter. Legacy appends 'Param' suffix to avoid potential conflicts with the options interface property.
archived_tasks.description: Constructor parameter is named 'endpoint' instead of 'endpointParam'. Minor naming convention difference.

archived_tasks.title: JSDoc Comments: Expanded @param Tags and Description Text Changes
archived_tasks.description: The new emitter generates different JSDoc content — expanded @param annotations, 'Optional parameters for the X operation' instead of 'Optional parameters.', removal of eslint-disable comments, multi-line formatting. Review if legacy JSDoc format must be matched exactly.
archived_tasks.legacy_behavior: Minimal JSDoc: '/** Optional parameters. */' with eslint-disable comments, single-line format.
archived_tasks.new_behavior: Expanded JSDoc with @param tags, operation-specific descriptions, multi-line format.
archived_tasks.decision: Accept improvement. Expanded JSDoc with @param tags and operation-specific descriptions is better documentation. No need to match legacy minimal JSDoc.
